global.title: Oil Rises Above $85 a Barrel as Gulf Clash Escalates - The New York Times
global.summary: Oil climbed above $85 as U.S.-Iran fighting intensified and ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz fell sharply, with commercial vessels hit and crews endangered.
global.whyItMatters: Rising energy shocks can deepen anxiety, strain household budgets, and shape prayers and preaching around peace, fear, and economic pressure.

global.title: Trump Says Fighting With Iran Has Resumed as He Orders Blockade and Tolls - The New York Times
global.summary: The U.S.-Iran cease-fire has effectively collapsed, with renewed strikes, a reinstated naval blockade, and new shipping tolls proposed for the Strait of Hormuz.
global.whyItMatters: This is a major escalation with humanitarian, geopolitical, and economic consequences that pastors may need to address in prayer and public leadership.

global.title: European shares edge lower amid renewed US-Iran tensions - Reuters
global.summary: European markets slipped as investors weighed renewed U.S.-Iran hostilities and Tehran's shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz, which pushed oil prices higher.
global.whyItMatters: Financial instability often reaches congregations through job insecurity, inflation, and stress, making this worth watching pastorally.

canadian.title: Wildfire Smoke Spreads Across the Northeast as Temperatures Spike - The New York Times
canadian.summary: Canadian wildfire smoke is drifting into the U.S. Northeast while a heat dome brings dangerous temperatures across southern Canada, including alerts in Ontario.
canadian.whyItMatters: Wildfire and heat concerns remain a national pastoral issue, affecting health, vulnerable people, and church readiness for care and community support.

canadian.title: Daily 5 report for July 14: Canadian dealers were warming up to Chinese EVs. Now, some have cold feet - Automotive News
canadian.summary: Canadian auto dealers are rethinking interest in Chinese EVs as trade uncertainty grows ahead of U.S.-Mexico discussions on USMCA rules and tariffs.
canadian.whyItMatters: Trade uncertainty can ripple into affordability, jobs, and consumer confidence in Canada, all of which affect families in the pews.

persecutedChurch.title: Church of England votes to hear Palestinian Christians on Israeli genocide in Gaza - Middle East Eye
persecutedChurch.summary: The Church of England's General Synod voted to hear testimony from Palestinian Christians through Kairos Palestine, despite significant backlash and debate.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It highlights the witness and suffering of Christians in conflict zones and may help inform prayer, discernment, and careful pastoral conversation.

persecutedChurch.title: Kuwait says worker injured in drone strike on drilling platform - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: Regional attacks linked to the widening Iran conflict now include a drone strike on a Kuwaiti drilling platform, underscoring the spread of instability across the Gulf.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: As conflict broadens across the region, churches should be alert to the vulnerability of minority communities and the need for informed intercession.

leadership.title: Your Pace Becomes Culture: The Pressure Signal Teams Copy - Forbes
leadership.summary: This piece argues that leaders set the emotional and operational tempo of their teams, and that unmanaged urgency eventually becomes burnout culture.
leadership.whyItMatters: Helpful for pastoral leadership: your pace, availability, and recovery habits quietly disciple staff and volunteers.

goodNews.title: Good News: Soccer fan earns World Cup tickets with $2 chores for years - NBC News
goodNews.summary: A 10-year-old soccer fan earned World Cup tickets for himself and his mom after patiently saving chore money over several years.
goodNews.whyItMatters: A simple story of perseverance, family love, and delayed gratification that can brighten a heavy news cycle.
